summary: East-oriented astronomical calendrical instrument from the Ica/Pisco coast. paqa (east/dawn) x3 = triple orientation. MIRROR STRUCTURE: Block 1 (80d) = Block 3 (80d) flanking central Block 2 (203d ≈ Scorpio visibility season at -14° lat). chaqa sv=40 at center = Pleiades invisibility period (~40d). Total L1 = 374d ≈ 1× Saturn synodic (378d, error -4d = 1.1%). Header: chaki(Scorpio) + [8-3-3-3]×2 periodic pattern. Value 16×6 with spacing pattern 1-3 repeating in both symmetric blocks. Dating: 3 Mode C candidates (1454/1456/1461), LOW-MEDIUM confidence. 53 cords, 10 STRING, 100% hits. Museum für Völkerkunde, Berlin.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  53 cords. 10 STRING (19%, 100% hits), 42 INT, 1 EMPTY.
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, depth L2. 8 clusters. Colors: B(30=57%), W(19), GA(4).
  Provenance: Between Ica and Pisco. Museum: Völkerkunde, Berlin.

VOCABULARY
  paqa x3 (east/dawn, C18/C45/C49) — TRIPLE orientation. sv=10, 10, 0.
  chaki x1 (Scorpio, C2 L2, sv=10) — thesis in header tree.
  maqa x1 (eclipse, C20 L2) — PAIRED with paqa = eclipse at dawn.
  chaqa x1 (bridge/naqa?, C27, sv=40) — highest sv.
  chiqa x1 (truth, C50, sv=10) — verification.
  taqa x1 (boundary, C51) — closing boundary.
  chi x2 (ki suffix, L2).

STRUCTURE
  Header (C1+16 L2 subs): C1=INT(1). L2: chaki(Scorpio), chi×2, values 8,8,3,3,3...
  Block 1 (C18-C26): paqa(east) + L2:maqa(eclipse). Then 5× ~16 (regular).
  Block 2 (C27-C41): chaqa sv=40. Then 6×4 (regular).
  Block 3 (C42-C53): 3×16. paqa(east). paqa(east). chiqa(truth). taqa(boundary).
  Value 16 × 6 = fixed count. Value 4 × 6 = secondary count.

NARRATIVE — THE DAWN OBSERVER
  East. Three times *paqa* — dawn, the direction of sunrise. Every observation begins at dawn. The header opens with *chaki* (Scorpio) and pairs *paqa* with *maqa* (eclipse at dawn). Five cords carry value 16 — a fixed count, perhaps half a lunar month. Then *chaqa* with the highest prefix (40). The register continues with the same rhythms: fours and sixteens. The last three words: *paqa* (east), *chiqa* (truth), *taqa* (boundary). East, truth, boundary. The observer certified what he saw from the eastern horizon.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — East-oriented observation register
Between Ica and Pisco — Chincha coast
